title: crepis sancta subsp. nemausensis
intro: Crepis sancta subsp. nemausensis, also known as the Nemausensis Hawk's Beard or Alpine Hawk's Beard, is a subspecies of the Crepis sancta plant, which is a type of flowering plant belonging to the dandelion family. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain, and soothing digestive issues due to its medicinal properties. Crepis sancta subsp. nemausensis can be prepared in various herbal forms, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, to utilize its medicinal properties for therapeutic purposes.
